title: High-throughput image-based drug screening of Caenorhabditis elegans movement, development, and viability
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"gbpyf","text":"Caenorhabdtis elegans is an important model organism for identifying and studying druggable targets in a high-throughput manner [1–3]. We have previously described a modular pipeline for image processing and analysis for both parasitic and free-living worms [4]. Here, we detail the upstream assay set up for high throughput drug screens of the free-living nematode C. elegans.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":22},{"style":"italic","offset":366,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":128,"length":7},{"key":1,"offset":259,"length":3}],"data":{}},{"key":"e3mod","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3zcnh","text":"Following a standard bleaching protocol, embryos are hatched overnight and synchronized L1s are added to a 96-well plate. Concentrated Esherichia coli strain HB101 bacterial food [5] is added to each well to sustain growth for 48 hours along with compounds of interest. Plates are incubated for 48 hours until worms reach the young adult stage. While this assay has been optimized for image acquisition with an ImageXpress high-content imager, it can be adapted for use on similar imaging platforms. Analysis of images from an ImageXpress is seamless using wrmXpress [4], but other analysis software can be used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":135,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":179,"length":3},{"key":3,"offset":568,"length":3}],"data":{}},{"key":"0a1wr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E23BA446EF5C11EFB0B30A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://paperpile.com/c/UHIyj3/sus1+qk2j+LHsu"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E23BA4D2EF5C11EFB0B30A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://paperpile.com/c/UHIyj3/uf3D"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E23BAA7FEF5C11EFB0B30A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://paperpile.com/c/UHIyj3/jHJw"}},"3":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E23BAABDEF5C11EFB0B30A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://paperpile.com/c/UHIyj3/uf3D"}}}}

steps: Streak HB101 from frozen stock onto LB agar plate (use plate within a week) and grow overnight at 37ºC
Inoculate 1 colony in 50 mL LB for 16 hr at 37ºC, with shaking (220 rpm)
Inoculate 500 µL of overnight culture in 500 mL of LB at 37ºC, 220 rpm for 13 hours (OD ~ 0.8 -0.9 for HB101)
Transfer the bacterial overnight culture to 8 x 50 mL tubes
Pre-weigh 2 of the 8 tubes and record weight on tube
Centrifuge for 10 min at 3,000 x g
Discard LB and repeat steps 4 & 5 until all bacteria is pelleted
Discard LB and add 40 mL of autoclaved H2O to 2 tubes (of 8)
Vortex to resuspend bacterial pellet
Sequentially transfer volume to 4 other tubes and vortex to resuspend

This combines the pellets of 10 tubes into the 2 pre-weighed tubes Note: If the pellet is large, 20mL can be added initially to get the pellet into solution, add up to 40mL after pellet is resuspended
Centrifuge for 10 min at 3,000 x g
Discard H2O and resuspend in 40 mL of autoclaved H20 via vortexing
Centrifuge for 10 min at 3,000 x g
Discard H2O and weigh the tubes to calculate the bacterial pellet weight
Re-suspend and combine pellets to a final concentration of 50 mg/mL in K-medium

Pellet total weight = X grams; Total resuspension volume = 20*X mL of K-medium Example: 1.8g pellet should be resuspended in 36 mLs (= 36 x 1 mL aliquots) Thoroughly vortex and then shake for a half hour at room temperature Aliquot into 1 mL units and store at -80ºC (label box with “HB101 HTA” and date) Note : Aliquots are 20x stocks. 5 µL of these aliquots are added into wells with a total volume of 100 µL. Each 1 mL bacteria stock tube can be used to feed two entire 96-well plates. Plates are fed on day one of the assay. Expected Yield : This protocol typically yields ~200 mL of 50 mg/mL HB101 stock, aliquoted into 200 x 1 mL tubes, which can feed a total of approximately 400 96-well plates.

Generate 10 cm NGM plates of gravid adults via picking 50 L4s to NGM plates 96 hours before bleaching, selecting transgenic worms if necessary. Alternatively, chunk to 10 cm NGM plates 72 hours before bleach.
Bleach (1 strain per 15 mL tube) when most adult worms are gravid ( bleach protocol)
Resuspend embryos in final volume of 2 mL M9
Titer embryos
Briefly mix by inverting tube
Count 1 x 5 µL aliquot
Add K media such that there are 5 embryos/µL
Count 3 x 5 µL aliquot
Add K media such that there are 3 embryos/µL
Hatch embryos for 12 - 20 hours at room temperature by nutating at setting speed 13 on the plate rocker or shaking at 180 RPM
After hatching count 10 x 5 µL aliquots on a glass slide Note: if you see too many carcasses, let tubes settle before drawing off L1s from the top.
Titer to 1 L1/µL in K media
Transfer titered L1s to a 25 mL trough
Using a multichannel pipette add 50 µL of titered L1s per well (50 L1s/well)
Using a multichannel pipette add 50 µL of HKM per well (final HB101 [2.5 mg/mL] )
To make HKM add 500 µL HB101 stock + 4.5 mL K media + 5 µL KAN stock (50 mg/mL, final 25 µg/mL) into a 50 mL trough
Add 1 µL of 100x drug or control per well
Alternatively, worms and HKM can be added directly to assay ready drug plates
Seal drug plates with breathable film
Assemble and place drug plates in humid chamber:
Clean (rinse thoroughly with ethanol and distilled water) plastic storage box
Place damp paper towels at the bottom of the box
Stack sealed drug plates into box
Close box and seal with parafilm
Place box into incubator at 20ºC, shaking at 180 rpm
Incubate for 48 hours
Motility:
Remove plates from shaking incubator and remove breathable strips
Wipe bottom of the plate with ethanol and kimwipe followed by a dry kimwipe
Take transmitted light videos using the ImageXpress or equivalent
4x, 1 site, 5 timepoints (frames per well) at ~3.3 FPS
CellTox (live/dead staining):
Remove plates from shaking incubator and remove breathable strips if not already removed for other phenotypic endpoints
Prepare a 1:200 dilution of the CellTox stock into the provided buffer
Add 10 µL of CellTox to each well
Replace breathable strips and place lids on plates
Incubate for 30 minutes at 20°C, shaking at 180 rpm, protected from light exposure
Wash three times using a multichannel pipette
Add 200 µL of M9 to each well
Gently shake for ~10 seconds
Remove 200 µL
Wash 2 additional times
Add 250 µL of M9-NaAz stock (NaAz final = 50 mM)
Alternatively, use an AquaMax plate washer to dispense 200 µL M9 to each plate
Spin the plates for 30 seconds using the VWR plate spinner
After the spin has completed, carefully remove the plates
Use the plate washer to aspirate to 5 mm
Repeat once more
Add 280 µL with M9-NaAz stock (NaAz final = 50 mM)
Top-fill wells with M9 to reduce shading during imaging
Wipe bottom of the plate with ethanol and kimwipe followed by a dry kimwipe
Image using an ImageXpress
2x, 1 site, 1 time point, GFP (other wavelengths as needed)
Note: Transmitted light filter can be used during image acquisition if worm size is being measured.
Size:
Remove plates from shaking incubator and remove breathable strips
Wipe bottom of the plate with ethanol and kimwipe followed by a dry kimwipe.
Add 250 µL of M9-NaAz stock (NaAz final = 50 mM)
Top-fill wells with M9 to reduce shading during imaging
Image using an ImageXpress
1 site at 2x with appropriate filters wrmXpress can be used to analyze motility, viability, and size data.
If the volume is >7.5 mL transfer to a 50 mL conical tube or split among multiple tubes
Count embryos using 1 x 5 µL aliquots on a glass slide to ensure 3 embryos/µL
creator: Protocols Importer
